report_name: Laravel-Lang Supply Chain Attack Injects Credential Stealer into 233 Package Versions

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The attacker compromised 233 versions of the laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/attributes, and laravel-lang/http-statuses packages by creating malicious version tags that pointed to a forked repository containing a credential-stealing payload. The malicious code was injected into the 'src/helpers.php' file.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The second-stage payload was a comprehensive PHP script designed to steal a wide range of credentials from the compromised system, including those from password stores.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials in Files
attack_patterns.description: The credential-stealing malware specifically targeted credentials stored in files, such as .env files, cloud credentials (AWS, Google Cloud), database credentials, and SSH keys.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The initial dropper payload fingerprinted the compromised machine, gathering system information before sending it to the C2 server.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The C2 server at flipboxstudio.info delivered a second-stage payload to the compromised system after receiving the initial fingerprint data.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The malware communicated with its C2 server over web protocols to exfiltrate system information and download the second-stage payload.

malware.name: Laravel-Lang Credential Stealer
malware.description: A multi-stage PHP malware injected into Laravel-Lang packages. The first stage acts as a dropper, fingerprinting the system and contacting a C2 server. The second stage is a comprehensive credential-stealing script that exfiltrates .env files, cloud credentials, database credentials, and SSH keys.

identities.name: Laravel-Lang
identities.description: A popular open-source project providing language localization packages for the Laravel PHP framework. Three of its repositories (laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/attributes, laravel-lang/http-statuses) were compromised in a supply chain attack.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The code hosting platform where the attacker abused a feature to create malicious version tags pointing to a forked repository, enabling the supply chain attack.

identities.name: Packagist
identities.description: The main PHP package repository that indexed the malicious version tags and distributed the compromised Laravel-Lang packages to developers.

identities.name: Laravel
identities.description: The popular PHP web application framework for which the compromised Laravel-Lang packages provide translations.

infrastructure.name: flipboxstudio.info C2 Server
infrastructure.description: A command-and-control server used by the attacker to host and deliver the second-stage credential-stealing payload.

indicators.name: Laravel-Lang Stealer C2 Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Laravel-Lang Supply Chain Attack C2 DNS Query
id: 9b1b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the C2 domain associated with the Laravel-Lang supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://socket.dev/blog/laravel-lang-supply-chain-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-23
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'flipboxstudio.info'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network communication with the known C2 domain flipboxstudio.info used in the Laravel-Lang supply chain attack.

indicators.name: Suspicious PHP Dropper in Laravel-Lang Package
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PHP Dropper File in Laravel-Lang Package
id: a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4890-abcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects the creation of a 'helpers.php' file within the laravel-lang vendor directory, which was the injection point for a credential stealing dropper. 
  File content should be inspected for obfuscated code such as 'eval(base64_decode())'.
references:
  - https://socket.dev/blog/laravel-lang-supply-chain-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-23
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains|all:
      - '/vendor/laravel-lang/'
      - '/src/helpers.php'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the `src/helpers.php` file within the `vendor/laravel-lang/` directory, which was the file compromised in the supply chain attack. The file should be inspected for malicious content.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
